name: Saint Roch
primary_sources: Vita Sancti Rochi by Francesco Diedo

distinctive_features: plague sore or bubo on thigh
accompanied by a dog carrying a loaf of bread
visual_attributes.texture: fabric of pilgrim's tunic, smooth skin
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: standing or walking posture, often lifting tunic to show sore

domain_specific_visual.iconography: depicted with a staff and pilgrim's hat

functional_attributes.primary_function: patron saint invoked against the plague

domain_specific_functional.role_in_catholicism: venerated as a saint and protector

relationships.target: Dolo, Parma, Casamassima, Cisterna di Latina, Palagiano, Arboleas, Albanchez, Deba
relationships.description: Saint Roch is the patron saint of these towns.
Gottardo Pallastrelli's dog helped Saint Roch during his illness.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of protection against plagues and epidemics
primary_sources: Vita Sancti Rochi by Francesco Diedo

contrastive_constraints: NOT without the red cross birthmark
NOT without the dog carrying bread
